figures.caption: Fig. 1: GTASA pipeline. Generated GEST graphs are converted into videos and accompanying textual descriptions. From these texts, we generate synthetic videos using neural models. The resulting videos—produced both by our approach and by other methods—are evaluated Q1 and subsequently used to train video captioning models Q2 and to probe existing video encoders for their spatiotemporal understanding Q3.

Table 5: Video captioning results. H = Human, G = GEST then Real, R = Real-only, V = VEO then Real. H is assigned rank 1 for metrics. Average ranks across metrics and judges independently (lower is better).
Table 7: Balanced accuracy (%) on spatial probing tasks. Best per task in bold. Rnd = random baseline. VL3∗ = partial results (8/11 tasks completed before submission).
Table 1: Comparison with video datasets and synthetic platforms. Per clip, GESTEngine provides two orders of magnitude more spatial relations than Action Genome (31.6K vs 170 per clip).
